name: Lake Hillier
description: An Australian lake whose pink hue defies scientific explanation.

name: Glowworm Tunnel
description: An abandoned railroad tunnel is now filled with bioluminescent bugs.

name: Gippsland Lakes Bioluminescence
description: Australian Lake set aglow by Noctiluca Scintillans.

name: Paronella Park
description: A castle hand-built by a Spanish dreamer in the Australian tropical jungle.

name: Newnes Glow Worm Tunnel
description: Once the path of a railway tunnel, now home to thousands of glowing insects.

name: Redwood Forest Tree Art
description: Woven tree art gives this towering Australian woodland a touch of mystical whimsy.

name: William Ricketts Sanctuary
description: A collection of outdoor mystical statues paying homage to indigenous Australians and ecological stewardship.

name: Wave Rock
description: This gracefully curving formation looks like a massive wave that was suddenly turned to stone.

name: Wendy's Secret Garden
description: Built over an abandoned train yard, this hidden gem is full of winding paths and offers gorgeous views of Sydney Harbour.

name: SS Ayrfield
description: Nature is leading a successful mutiny on this century-old freighter.

name: The Giant Stairway
description: A massive stairway that almost wasn't leads to one of Australia's most scenic vistas.

name: Ball's Pyramid
description: This dramatic sea spire is home to the world's rarest insect and is part of the lost continent of Zealandia.

name: Flinders Street Station Ballroom
description: An abandoned jewel of a ballroom on the top floor of Australia’s busiest rail station echoes with the sounds of a bygone era.

name: Ashcombe Maze
description: Australia's largest traditional hedge maze features a Great Gnome Hunt.

name: Wunderkammer
description: The word 'wunderkammer' translates as 'wonder-chamber'. This Wunderkammer is both a museum and a retail store.

name: 'Forgotten Songs' Sound Sculpture
description: A collaboration of art and ornithology mourns the calls of Sydney's lost birds.

name: Whitehaven Beach
description: A small, but award-winning beach known for its literally spotless reputation.

name: Gould's Book Arcade
description: One of Australia's largest secondhand bookstores.

name: Eaglehawk Neck Tessellated Pavement
description: This extraordinary geological oddity resembles human-made pavement.

name: Lake McKenzie / Boorangoora
description: The only pollutant in the clear blue water is sunscreen.

name: Coober Pedy
description: An opal mining town in the middle of Australia where people live, worship, and shop underground.

name: Melbourne Storm Tunnels
description: The labyrinthine storm tunnels beneath the city of Melbourne are ruled by a band of urban explorers.

name: The Witches Garden
description: The largest collection of medicinal plants in Australia is also home to a witch who will show you how to use them.

name: Giant Pink Slugs of Mount Kaputar
description: The unique ecosystem atop an Australian mountain is home to some colorful inhabitants.

name: Museum of Human Disease
description: Australian museum dedicated to human health and lack thereof.

name: Cape Pillar Sea Cliffs
description: The tallest sea cliffs on the Southern Hemisphere are a climber's worst nightmare.

name: Wedding Cake Rock
description: A sparkling sandstone cliffside that's worth a visit while it still exists.

name: Mungo National Park
description: Prehistoric park filled with the fossils of dozens of extinct species including Australian megafauna.

name: Wineglass Bay
description: One of the best beaches in the entire world.

name: St. Kilda Pier Penguin Colony
description: You'll find the little birds tucked within the rocks of this Australian beach.

name: The Pinnacles
description: Alien rock formations grown from the sands of an Australian national park.

name: Royal Botanic Gardens, Sydney
description: Come for the flowers, stay for the bats!

name: Wolfe Creek Crater
description: A stray meteor left this picturesque hole in the Australian outback.

name: Loch Ard Gorge
description: A clear example of the process of erosion in action.

name: Purikura Photoland
description: People can't get enough of these adorable Japanese photobooths.

name: Horizontal Falls
description: This pair of Australian "waterfalls" appear to be falling straight across the land.

name: Melbourne Medical Museum
description: Australia's largest medical museum featuring a 19th-century pharmacy.

name: Fruit Bats of Yarra Bend Park
description: Is there such thing as too many fruit bats? Not anymore.

name: Sydney Observatory
description: This historic observatory contains the world's oldest refracting lens telescope.

name: The Twelve Apostles
description: Nine limestone rock stacks on Australia's "Shipwreck Coast."

name: The Stromatolites of Hamelin Pool
description: Living fossils in this remote Australian bay provide a glimpse of what Earth may have looked like 3.5 billion years ago.

name: Shell Beach
description: This Australian beach is composed of tiny shells.

name: Styx Valley Forest
description: Rainforest filled with record-holding eucalyptus.

name: The Butterfly Room
description: Hundreds of tiny dead butterfly eyes gaze down at diners in this Australian eatery.

name: The Lex and Ruby Graham Gardens
description: This secret Australian garden was built on love after a man planted a random bulb he found in the ocean.

name: Gnomesville
description: Thousands of lawn gnomes have infested this Australian roundabout.

name: Badu Mangroves
description: A boardwalk winds through this sliver of coastal nature hidden within Sydney.

name: Penguin Parade At Phillip Island
description: An unphotographable nightly march of tens of thousands of "fairy penguins" from sea to shore.

name: Museum of Underwater Art
description: A series of sculptures beneath the waves promote ocean conservation and support coral reefs.

name: Three Sisters
description: This trio of Australian rock spires may have inspired aboriginal legend or just modern tourism.

name: Atlantis Marine Park
description: An abandoned marine theme park has created a difficult experiment in dolphin rewilding.

name: Melbourne GlowGolf Docklands
description: Putt your way around Australia in a black light environment.

name: Bay of Fires
description: Aqua white sand highlighted with fiery red rocks.

name: Tasmazia and the Village of Lower Crackpot
description: A huge maze complex and whimsical miniature village in Tasmania.

name: Ancient Aboriginal Astronomy
description: An ancient rock engraving shows Aboriginals used their advanced knowledge of the stars to hunt and gather.

name: Magnetic Hill
description: When you get to Magnetic Hill park near the sign, put your vehicle in neutral gear, and watch it roll uphill...

name: Justice and Police Museum
description: This grim museum collects artifacts and evidence of some of the most sensational antipodean crimes.

name: Cage of Death
description: Visitors can swim with vicious predators in Crocosaurus Cove's plastic daredevil enclosure.

name: Umpherston Sinkhole
description: An incredible garden taking over the interior of a massive sinkhole.

name: Crabs of Christmas Island
description: The red crab exhibits one of the most spectacular migrations on earth, despite manmade obstacles.

name: Trim the Cat
description: The statue honors an adventurous seafaring cat.

name: Little Blue Lake
description: A beautiful, but toxic, lake formed by mining.

name: Cockatoo Island
description: Cockatoo Island is a Heritage-listed island in the middle of Sydney Harbour.

name: Macquarie Island
description: One of the only places where Earth’s mantle lies above water.

name: Undara Lava Tubes
description: White cockroaches and other cave species occupy this massive series of lava tubes.

name: Devil's Marbles
description: Clusters of huge boulders in the Australian Outback.

name: The Johnston Collection
description: A remarkable lifelong collection of antiques and art preserved in a historic Melbourne townhouse.

name: Port Arthur Penal Colony
description: Relics of Australia's convict past, and a modern scene of unimaginable horror.

name: Capricorn Caves
description: A wonder of natural limestone formations etched by millions of years of natural interaction between rainfall and rock and air.

name: Bungle Bungle Range
description: These Australian rock formations look like Martian beehives that have grown up out of the landscape.

name: The Pitch Drop Experiment
description: Started nearly 100 years ago, this science experiment just keeps on going, ever so slowly.

name: Aboriginal Carving of a Whale
description: An ancient engraving by the Cammeraygal people was uncovered in the transformation of a North Sydney industrial site.

name: Coop's Shot Tower
description: This historic Melbourne bullet factory is fully encased in a massive glass and steel cone.

name: Kiama Blowhole
description: This massive water spout has been luring visitors for hundreds of years, some to their death.

name: Old Melbourne Gaol
description: Once housing children next to hardened criminals this historic jail once displayed Ned Kelly's skull until it was stolen.

name: Mad Max 2 Museum
description: This shrine to film's most iconic wasteland wanderer is located in the very outback where the movies were shot.

name: Eric the Pliosaur
description: The bones of this aquatic dinosaur turned to opal over the centuries and the beast's remains were almost sold as jewelry.

name: The Enchanted Maze Garden
description: Largest maize maze in the Southern hemisphere built with GPS technology on more than two acres.

name: White Rabbit Gallery
description: It's said to be one of the most significant collections of contemporary Chinese art in the world.

name: Dreamer's Gate
description: One man's artistic vision is a small town government's legal battle.

name: Waverley Cemetery
description: A self-funded cemetery with a long list of inhabitants.

name: Crocodile Harry's Underground Nest & Dugout
description: This eccentric cave dwelling is bizarre even by the standards of a subterranean town.

name: Burning Mountain
description: 6000-year-old coal fire moves one meter per year.

name: The Nicholas Building
description: A beautiful 1920s tower filled with local hidden gems.

name: Inside Australia
description: A series of eerie metal stick figures are spread out over four square miles on the bed of a huge salt lake in the Australian Outback.

name: Cow Up a Tree
description: This piece of Australian modern art is part joke and part history.

name: Bruno’s Art and Sculpture Garden
description: A forest full of mystical fantasy figures, nearly destroyed by recent fires.

name: SS Maheno
description: WWI hospital ship lost in a cyclone turns up on the coast of Fraser Island, where she remains rusted and forgotten.

name: Mortuary Station
description: A special train service transported Sydney's dead from this station to "Necropolis Receiving Houses" at Rookwood Cemetery.

name: Water Dragons of the Chinese Garden of Friendship
description: Native Australian lizards inhabit this garden celebrating Sydney and its sister city in China.

name: Hamelin Bay Beach
description: Incredible rock formations jut from the southern end of this spectacular stingray-viewing spot.

name: The Big Pineapple
description: A giant Australian fruit and tropical farm.

name: St Columba Falls
description: A granite waterfall cascading down is one of the highest multi-tiered falls in the state.

name: Gwalia Ghost Town
description: This once-booming gold mining hub was basically abandoned overnight.

name: Dularcha Rail Tunnel
description: This abandoned railroad tunnel is now home to a micro-colony of bats and a ghost train.

name: Mermaid Inlet Cliffs
description: These sea cliffs are an epic natural wonder.

name: Princess Theatre
description: One of the oldest theaters in Australia, it's also said to be haunted by a performer who died on stage.

name: Box Hill Brick Works
description: A local landmark that has become a hub of graffiti and urban exploration.

name: Museum of Old and New Art (MONA)
description: This controversial museum includes such detested exhibits as a chocolate suicide bomber and fecal-smelling digestion machine.

name: Lake Tyrrell
description: Victoria's largest salt lake is a favorite haunt for photographers and stargazers.

name: The Limelight Department
description: A cramped wooden attic that once housed one of the world's first movie studios.

name: Mabu Mabu Big Esso
description: Featuring Torres Strait Islander cuisine and Australia's first indigenous bar.

name: Strelley Pool
description: Microscopic fossils suggest Australia may be the birthplace of sulphur-based life forms.

name: Marree Man
description: Not a single witness can attest to the creation of the world's largest geoglyph.

name: Victoria Police Museum
description: Unusual finds in this shrine to local law enforcement include a 19th-century vampire-slaying kit.

name: Fairies Tree
description: A whimsical menagerie of creatures carved into a 300-year-old tree trunk.

name: Drunken Australian Parrots
description: The Australian lorikeets that like to tie one on in an annual drinking binge.

name: Gog and Magog
description: These mythological giants take time out of their busy day guarding a Victorian market to let you know what time it is.

name: National Gallery Sculpture Garden
description: Dozens of large-scale, creative, evocative curios from prominent artists dot the calm outdoor space around this major museum.

name: AC/DC Lane
description: A colorful street named for the legendary Australian rock band.

name: Melbourne Observatory
description: The city’s oldest gateway to the galaxy lies at the entrance to the Royal Botanical Gardens.

name: The Bunyip of Berkeley's Creek
description: A friendly monster lurks outside the State Library Victoria.

name: Lord Howe Island's Time
description: In a strange timezone anomaly, Daylight Saving Time here sets the clocks forward just 30 minutes.

name: Z Ward
description: A perfectly preserved abandoned criminal asylum in South Australia.

name: Magnetic Island
description: After much poking and prodding, this island is decidedly not a magnetic anomaly, but it sure is pretty.

name: Something Wild
description: This store offers tastes of Aboriginal Australia, with some contemporary and global twists.

name: Hyams Beach
description: The sand is such a bright white that on sunny days you practically need sunglasses to look at the ground.

name: Skull Rock (Cleft Island)
description: A unique rock formation that only a few have managed to venture inside.

name: Bicheno Blowhole
description: Granite rocks that concentrate waves into powerful jets.

name: Tasmania's 'Town of Murals'
description: The once-dying town of Sheffield, Tasmania has reinvented itself as a village of painted wonders.

name: Gold Coast Wax Museum and Chamber of Horrors
description: The largest wax museum in the southern hemisphere features a grizzly chamber of horrors.

name: Streets Beach
description: Brisbane's manmade urban beach has enough water to fill five Olympic-sized swimming pools.

name: Empire of Atlantium
description: The smallest country in Australia is a principled little thing with a friendly emperor.

name: Cairns Library Bats
description: These flying foxes dangle from the trees around the library like fuzzy black fruit.

name: Hutt Lagoon
description: A massive concentration of algae is responsible for this lake's pink hue.

name: Santos Museum of Economic Botany
description: A wealth of plants and horticultural curiosities fill this charmingly antique museum.

name: Grave of the Tamam Shud Man
description: Dedicated to an "Unknown Man," this grave holds either the remains of a lovelorn suicide or a Cold War spy.

name: Ned Kelly's Armour
description: The bullet-banged DIY suit of armour belonging to Australia’s most famous outlaw.

name: Sydney Bus Museum
description: Ninety years of Sydney buses under one roof.

name: Daintree Entomological Museum
description: Australia's largest collection of rare and bizarre insects.

name: Mailbox Art Space
description: Tiny artwork tucked within glass letterboxes form this hidden gem of an art gallery.

name: Boolboonda Train Tunnel
description: The longest manmade tunnel in Queensland is now a beautiful trail, home to a special colony of bent-wing bats.

name: The South Fremantle Power Station
description: The ruins of a once prosperous power station have become a mouldering seaside gem.

name: Lark Quarry Dinosaur Trackways
description: These tracks are believed to be from a panicked dinosaur stampede.

name: The Big Prawn
description: There's nothing shrimpy about the world's largest artificial prawn.

name: Captain Cook's Cottage
description: The 18th-century home was disassembled into 253 cases and brought all the way from the other side of the world.

name: Gore Hill Cemetery
description: One of Sydney's oldest graveyards hides in plain sight behind a hospital.

name: The Academy of Taxidermy
description: One of Australia's largest collections of preserved animals is on display in a historic theater.

name: Ballandean Pyramid
description: A huge stone pyramid built for fun.

name: Larry The Red Big Lobster
description: A massive metal lobster welcomes tourists to a roadside restaurant.

name: The Big Mango
description: One of Australia's famed "Big Things," this giant fruit was once stolen as part of a publicity stunt.

name: Mystery Craters
description: The 25-million-year-old cavities have perplexed people for decades.

name: Japanese War Cemetery and Garden
description: Final resting place for hundreds of prisoners of war.

name: Marineland Melanesia
description: Home of the world's largest crocodile.

name: The Ruins of the Beaumaris Zoo
description: The abandoned remains of a once popular zoo, and the grave of the last known Tasmanian Tiger.

name: Bundaberg Botanic Gardens
description: Peanut butter trees and miracle berries are just the beginning.

name: The Birdsville Hotel
description: A historic hotel on the edge of the Australian outback.

name: The Giant Koala
description: Popular tourist destination now stands as a memorial to Sam the koala.

name: Dingo Fence
description: The longest fence in the world stretches over 3,000 miles to keep the dingoes out.

name: Gosse Bluff
description: A spectacular impact crater in the rugged heart of Central Australia.

name: Broken Hill Sculpture Symposium
description: Twelve sculptures designed to make their best showing with the coming or going of the sun.

name: Odd Mountain
description: A small curiosity shop with items ranging from antique medical supplies to taxidermied animals.

name: Skylab’s Remains
description: A tiny museum in Esperance, Australia, displays a bunch of space debris from NASA’s Skylab.

name: Newcastle City Morgue Echo Spot
description: By standing on a particular paving stone in the courtyard, you'll hear an echo that shouldn't be.

name: Kangaroo Island Bee Sanctuary
description: An island off the coast of South Australia is home to the last purebred Ligurian bees.

name: Georges Head Battery
description: The yawning remains of a complex naval fortress still haunt this Australian shore.

name: Big Potato
description: Concrete spud looks like the North American Sebago.

name: Big Avocado
description: Giant fruit signaling the entrance to a tropical paradise.

name: Abbotsford Convent
description: This 19th-century convent is now a flourishing 21st-century hub for art.

name: Tasmanian Devil Unzoo
description: The world's first cageless zoo is working to save endangered Tasmanian devils.

name: South Australian Museum
description: The largest collection of Indigenous Australian artifacts in the world, and the rare museum where wood-and-glass cabinets have trumped interactive exhibits.

name: Nettle Cave Tour in Klingon
description: The first tourist attraction to offer tours for interstellar tourists.

name: Easey's
description: A train car sitting on a rooftop makes an unlikely and picturesque setting for dinner.

name: Marionette Fob Watch
description: Every hour a secret compartment drops from this giant pocket watch, revealing a lovely surprise.

name: Queen Adelaide Restaurant
description: Dine aboard the historic Ghan train while retracing early explorers' journey through the Outback.

name: McClelland Gallery & Sculpture Park
description: For better or worse, this whimsical sculpture garden was a favorite place of Rupert Murdoch's.

name: Elvis Presley Memorial Garden
description: The King is honored by an elaborate grotto on a continent he never visited.

name: Disappointment Rock
description: No one seems to know who suffered the disappointment at this particular place.

name: Big Kookaburra
description: This 4.5-meter-tall statue of a native Australian bird marks Hydro Aluminium's 40th year in the area.

name: Derby Boab 'Prison Tree'
description: Critics argue this boab tree has been mislabeled for the sake of dark tourism, undermining its spiritual significance.

name: Commissariat Store
description: Its most grisly object is a jar full of severed fingers allegedly cut off by prisoners to avoid work.

name: Organ Pipes
description: Cooled, fractured lava formed this geometric rock feature.

name: Totem Pole
description: Many rock climbers have come to conquer this slender sea stack, with various degrees of success.

name: Titanic Theatre Restaurant
description: Enjoy all the trappings from 1912 aboard this Titanic-themed restaurant.

name: Australian Monument to the Great Irish Famine
description: A monument to the 4,114 Irish orphan girls who arrived in Australia between 1848 and 1850.

name: Dinosaur Garden
description: Outside the National Dinosaur Museum, dozens of dinosaur statues and sculptures claim the landscape.

name: Dinosaur Garden
description: Outside the National Dinosaur Museum, dozens of dinosaur statues and sculptures claim the landscape.

name: Archer's Arcadia
description: Arcade chock-full of old timey, whimsical, hand-cranked automata and peepshows.

name: Tumuli Lava Blisters
description: These naturally occurring but rare geological formations are each about the size of a small house.

name: Polly Woodside
description: This historic tall ship traveled 1.5 million miles around the globe, and once did duty as the mythical pirate ship in Peter Pan.

name: Seized Endangered Species Exhibit
description: An exhibit of endangered species confiscated from smugglers and passengers.

name: Flinders Street Railway Station Photo Booth
description: This classic photo booth was saved from destruction by the local community.

name: Henbury Meteorites Conservation Park
description: Curious crater remnants in the middle of the Australian outback.

name: Black Mountain
description: Legend says entire herds of cattle have disappeared within this boulder-strewn landscape.

name: The Bibbulmun Track
description: A beautiful hike through national parks, forests and beaches in Western Australia.

name: Phar Lap
description: Taxidermy of the racing horse with a huge heart, who inspired hope in the Depression.

name: Parkes Radio Telescope
description: This giant dish located in a sheep paddock was the primary receiver of Apollo 11 TV transmissions.

name: Principality of Hutt River
description: A micronation of epic proportions.

name: Fire Services Museum of Victoria
description: A firefighting museum housed in a historic station whose Lookout Tower watches over Melbourne.

name: Big Poo
description: At five meters long, that is one big piece of foam poop.

name: Olive Pink Botanic Garden
description: Olive’s garden serves up a seemingly endless helping of outback delights.

name: Phillip Island Koala Conservation Reserve
description: A conservation sanctuary for rescued koalas, some of which were saved from devastating Australian bushfires.

name: Lambert Centre of Australia
description: Finding the exact middle of a giant land mass is hard to determine, but this spot managed to do the trick.

name: Bottle Cap Tree
description: Hundreds of people walk past this palm tree every day without spotting its secret.

name: Heard and McDonald Islands
description: Australia's only active volcanoes and highest peak are located on these remote islands thousands of miles away from the mainland.

name: Manly Quarantine Station
description: The Victoria-era quarantine station for Sydney.

name: The Shine Dome
description: This copper-coated, multi-arched dome is surrounded by a moat and lights up at night.

name: Lake Thetis
description: One of the few places in the world home to living stromatolites.

name: Woolshed Falls
description: This gold mine–turned–swimming hole is the perfect summer picnic spot, immersed in nature and history.

name: Pioneer Settlement
description: Australia's first open air museum and antique 3D theater.

name: Charles Dickens Statue
description: This rare effigy of the author spent years lost in storage.

name: Larry La Trobe Statue
description: Melbourne's favorite bronze dog.

name: Paddington Reservoir Gardens
description: A former drinking water storage reservoir transformed into a sunken garden.

name: The Queen's Letter
description: This letter written by Queen Elizabeth II in 1986 won't be opened until 2085.

name: Rookwood Cemetery
description: The largest necropolis in the Southern Hemisphere.

name: Giant Ram
description: Everything about this over-size novelty animal is huge including its testicles.

name: The Leaning Tower of Gingin
description: The world's "leaniest" tower encourages you to test the gravity of its situation.

name: Wittenoom
description: A town whose main resource was blue asbestos... what could possibly go wrong?

name: Budj Bim Cultural Landscape
description: The Gunditjmara Aboriginal people of Australia began construction on this vast aquacultural complex 6,600 years ago.

name: Queensland Police Museum
description: It's home to the taxidermy remains of Peter, the dog who helped convict a murderer.

name: Corrigan Suspension Bridge
description: Designed to provide fantastic views of the surrounding rainforest.

name: The Jade Buddha for Universal Peace
description: Largest Buddha carved from gemstone-quality jade in the world, on a world tour through 2011.

name: Beehive Casemate
description: A defensive fortification carved into a cliff near Sydney Harbour.

name: Eden Killer Whale Museum
description: A microcosm of the whaling industry in a small Australian town.

name: Montsalvat
description: Australia's oldest artists' colony.

name: The Shark and Ray Rescue Centre
description: Once an aquarium, this sanctuary now rescues sharks and rays from fishing nets.

name: Big Merino
description: Three-story concrete ram with a permanent gift shop.

name: Sirius Building
description: Though considered an eyesore by some, this boxy Brutalist high-rise plays a significant role in Sydney's past.

name: Seahorse World
description: An active seahorse farm is currently saving Tasmania's handfish from extinction.

name: Lake Amadeus
description: This large lake in Central Australia contains an estimated 600 million tons of salt.

name: Coast Cemetery
description: Hidden among popular Sydney golf courses is a little remembered graveyard populated by smallpox victims.

name: Big Bicycle
description: Roadside attraction outside of a recycling center.

name: Big Bench
description: Located on top of mines that run through Broken Hill.

name: Old Tailem Town
description: This pioneer village is like a time capsule of 19th-century Australia.

name: Buchan Caves Reserve
description: These 400 million-year-old caves feature honeycomb limestone formations.

name: Ramalama Book Exchange
description: Leaning shelves and books galore, this location is a book lover's utopia.

name: Darwin Oil Storage Tunnels
description: World War II ended before these tunnels built to protect vital oil from Japanese bombs were ever used.

name: Big Blue Heeler
description: Standing in celebration of where the cattle dog was bred.

name: Lake Bumbunga
description: This salt lake in South Australia features seasonal color changes and its very own loch monster.

name: HMVS Cerberus
description: Australia's only intact colonial warship never saw action before being turned into a scenic wreck.

name: Museum of Lands, Mapping, and Surveying
description: Its collection highlights the monumental tasks undertaken to map Queensland.

name: Beautiful Betsy Crash Site
description: For nearly 50 years this airplane that crashed during World War II remained missing in the remote Australian backcountry.

name: St. John’s Cemetery
description: The oldest European burial ground in Australia.

name: 'Mr. Lizard and Gumnut Baby' Statue
description: Children's author May Gibbs's beloved stories about the tiny, human-like "Gumnut Babies" are immortalized in bronze.

name: Big Bull
description: Large enough to house an internal gift shop.

name: Artist's Lane
description: This previously dilapidated alleyway has been transformed into one of Melbourne's finest hidden art locations.

name: South Lawn Car Park
description: A unique underground car park that has become a popular filming location.

name: Big Playable Guitar
description: Record-holding guitar meant to promote country.

name: Stone Church Fish-and-Chips Shop
description: Worship at the altar of seafood at this shop housed inside the town's oldest stone church.

name: White Cliffs
description: Australia's first opal field led to a mining rush in the 1890s.

name: ‘The World Turns’
description: The sculpture shows a water rat turning the world upside-down, landing an elephant on its head.

name: Raising the Rattler Pole
description: Not the result of an accident, hurricane, or aliens, this is an eye-popping tribute to the Melbourne tram.

name: Webb Bridge
description: This modern pedestrian bridge on the Yarra River was modeled after an Aboriginal fish trap.

name: C.Y. O'Connor Horse and Rider
description: This drowning bronze monument marks the spot where one of Australia's great engineers died by suicide.

name: West Wallabi Island
description: The oldest known European structures in Australia are the remnants of a story filled with mutiny, murder, jealousy and greed.

name: Fairy Park
description: This slightly spooky yet nostalgic theme park claims to be the first of its kind in Australia.

name: The Big Trout
description: This giant trout was made by a singular fisherman who wanted to honor his local trade.

name: Big Cherries
description: Honoring the annual National Cherry Festival.

name: Mutonia Sculpture Park
description: Lonely industrial sculptures in South Australia created by a mechanic turned artist.

name: Thylacine Taxidermy
description: A rare specimen of an iconic extinct animal in downtown Sydney.

name: The Gothic Bank
description: This Melbourne bank has unique Gothic-style architectural features and is one of the most majestic operating bank branches in the world.

name: Crystal World Exhibition Centre
description: A potpourri of rocks, fossils, and crystals is rounded out by a dinosaur garden and a 15-foot great white shark.

name: Sydney's Original Doll Hospital
description: Over two million dolls have been healed during this hospital's century in business.

name: Coalseam Conservation Park
description: Created as a coal reserve, it is today a natural park with stunning wildflowers, ancient marine fossils, and remnants of a 19th century mine.

name: Raymond Island Koala Trail
description: This area was created to save the region's koala population.

name: St. Helena Island
description: The ruins of Brisbane’s own Alcatraz now sit in the middle of a national park.

name: The Burra Homestead
description: A small Australian farmhouse became an international icon after appearing on a Midnight Oil album cover.

name: Spiky Bridge
description: This historic Australian bridge was built by convicts with giant stone spikes to prevent cattle from falling off the sides.

name: Grandad's Magical Wonderland
description: This whimsical garden filled with fairies and incredible creatures has been enchanting children—and adults—since the 1970s.

name: Phar Lap's Heart
description: The gigantic preserved heart of a champion racehorse.

name: Big Oyster
description: Homage to the local produce now serves as a car dealer.

name: Timeball Tower
description: An obsolete form of timekeeping still marks the hour in this adapted 19th century lighthouse.

name: Rainbow Valley
description: This sacred site lives up to its name with its majestic, color-splashed sandstone formations.

name: Cactus and Succulent Garden
description: Hidden within Sydney’s lush Royal Botanic Garden is a walled cactus rockery and the site of Australia's first zoo.

name: Pooseum
description: A small museum dedicated to taking the taboo out of all things related to poo.

name: Lambing Flat Chinese Tribute Garden
description: Tribute to the Chinese people of Young and Australia.

name: Squeaky Beach
description: The spherical grains of white quartz sand give every step on this Australian beach a distinctive sound.

name: 'A Day Out'
description: These bronze sculptures sow whimsy by hamming it up on a pedestrian mall.

name: Luna Park Melbourne
description: Home to the oldest operating roller coaster in the Southern Hemisphere.

name: Sunshine Lane
description: A hidden lane full of top-quality street art.

name: Alligator Gorge
description: The evolution of a simple creek cutting its way through an ochre mountain side.

name: The Big Rocking Horse
description: You can climb to the top of the world's largest rocking horse.

name: The Old Gum Tree
description: The colony of South Australia was proclaimed on a hot December day under a distinctively arched gum tree.

name: Clifton Hill Shot Tower
description: The largest shot tower in Australia is a unique reminder of industrial ammunitions history.

name: The Nobbys Gunpowder Tunnels
description: A series of gun powder chambers currently hidden from view within Nobbys Headland in Newcastle, Australia.

name: Big Mosquito
description: Modeled after the big, ferocious Hexham Grey mosquito.

name: Canberra Deep Space Communication Complex
description: It's home to the antenna that received the first images of Neil Armstrong walking on the moon.

name: Big Apple
description: Entrance to a famous orchard with nine types of apples.

name: Beechworth Asylum
description: This former psychiatric hospital was once one of the largest in Australia.

name: Big Axe
description: Giant wood sculpture in Kew first constructed in 1979.

name: Frederick Valentich Memorial Plaque
description: A plaque at the lighthouse where Frederick Valentich made his last radio contact before he vanished without a trace.

name: The Sphinx Hotel
description: A taste of Egypt on the outskirts of Geelong.

name: Mrs. Macquarie’s Chair
description: A 19th-century perch for spotting sailing ships now boasts one of the most iconic views in the Southern Hemisphere.

name: Woodsreef Asbestos Mine
description: Asbestos pit mine abandoned in 1983 is still dangerous thirty years later.

name: Australian National University Classics Museum
description: A little-known museum of Ancient Roman and Ancient Greek artifacts.

name: 'The Public Purse'
description: This giant, granite purse is one of Melbourne's most unique and distinctive pieces of public art.

name: Model Tudor Village
description: A historic model village was gifted from residents of London to Melbourne as thanks for their help during World War II.

name: Marble Masterpiece
description: A miniature cathedral made of 20,948 tiny pieces of marble.

name: Marble Masterpiece
description: A miniature cathedral made of 20,948 tiny pieces of marble.

name: Tin Horse Highway
description: There's a lot of horsing around on this lonely stretch of Australian highway.

name: Big Bunch of Bananas
description: Roadside display for a popular fruit and vegetable stand.

name: The Shot Tower Historic Site
description: For a century, the tallest building in Tasmania was a tower built to manufacture ammunition.

name: Spotswood Pumping Station
description: A youth-centric science center hides an industrial triumph.

name: Garden Island Ship's Graveyard
description: The sullen remains of vessels abandoned in the Port Adelaide River.

name: Upside-Down Charles La Trobe Statue
description: This strange portrayal of the state's first lieutenant-governor literally stands on its head.

name: Geelong Bollards
description: More than 100 colorful residents stand post in this seaside Australian city.

name: Remarkable Cave
description: It's said that visitors can "see" Tasmania inside this cave.

name: Hobart Rivulet
description: This urban stream flowing underneath Tasmania's capital city is also an unofficial gallery for street artists.

name: Nepean Island
description: This tiny uninhabited islet off the coast of Norfolk Island was formed by blowing sand from nearby dunes.

name: Bunjil's Shelter
description: This ancient rock shelter features cave art and is one of the most culturally important sites in the country.

name: Big Lamb
description: Monument to the area's potato and lamb industries.

name: Banjawarn Station
description: An outback test site for the Aum Shinrikyo doomsday cult.

name: David Roche House Museum
description: A vast and opulent collection of art and show dog memorabilia collected over one man's lifetime.

name: Big Bowl
description: Australian holiday town hosts world's largest bowling ball.

name: Deep Rock Swimming Hole
description: The gathering point of an old Melbourne swimming club was site of a historic, world-record dive in 1918.

name: Big Bowl
description: Australian holiday town hosts world's largest bowling ball.

name: Pooh Bear's Corner
description: A small mountain cave tucked away along Kings Highway has become a shrine to the beloved character.

name: Gnome Holm
description: In this Red Rock forest, a small enclave plays host to a collection of wacky figurines.

name: Nullarbor Links
description: The holes in the world's longest golf course are in a series of different towns.

name: Tasmans Arch
description: A natural rock bridge towers above the sea.

name: Lake Clifton Thrombolites
description: An impressive site containing some of the earth’s rarest living fossils.

name: Titanic Bandsmen Memorial
description: A modest park memorial pays tribute to one of the Titanic's most revered storylines.

name: Long Tunnel Extended Gold Mine
description: A rare chance to enter an original gold mine from the 1800s.

name: Boot Hill
description: Thousands of used shoes create a unique public art exhibit along this old paddock fence.

name: Big Beer Can
description: Can of beer on top of a Cobar hotel with a world record.

name: Mount Kosciuszko Summit
description: The tallest mountain in mainland Australia.

name: Big Chook
description: Fiberglas monument to Moonbi's poultry industry.

name: Gympie Bone Museum
description: Australia's first and only museum dedicated exclusively to bones.

name: Newman's Nursery Ruins
description: The haunting remains of a beautiful old plant nursery founded in the 1850s.

name: The Dolphin Fountain
description: Somewhat controversial when first unveiled, this gathering of sea creatures has become a hidden gem.

name: Red Rock
description: One of the youngest volcanic eruption sites in Australia.

name: Big Murray Cod
description: Fiberglass fish built in 1968 after months of fundraising.

name: Noojee Trestle Bridge
description: This remnant of an old railroad is the tallest surviving wooden trestle bridge in Victoria.

name: Keith Haring Mural

name: Burdekin Falls Dam
description: Queensland's largest dam includes a winding spillway that offers drivers a mesmerizing sight.

name: Big Golden Guitar
description: Monument that stands 40 feet and weighs 1,000 pounds.

name: Midden
description: In the shadow of the Sydney Opera House, Indigenous ingredients shine.

name: 'The Dancers'
description: The unique work of art celebrates the 30th anniversary of the 1988 World Expo.

name: Millaa Millaa Waterfall
description: The name for this waterfall was derived from the Aboriginal word used to describe a rainforest vine.

name: Cheviot Beach
description: This Australian beach became famous when Prime Minister Harold Holt disappeared from its shores.

name: The Big Owl
description: This roadside attraction became locally famous for its unintentionally phallic shape.

name: Waddamana Power Station
description: The decommissioned station is now a museum packed with antiquated hydroelectricity artifacts.

name: Southport Cable Hut
description: The Australian terminal of the Pacific Cable that linked the continent to North America.

name: Ned Kelly Animatronic Museum
description: A totally unique, animatronic depiction of the Australian gunslinger's last stand.

name: The Perth Mint
description: This Western Australian mint was established during the height of the Australian gold rush.

name: 'Emblem'
description: A colorful sculpture of an Australian symbol patrols an otherwise ordinary traffic island.

name: Karlstein Castle Replica
description: This mini version of the famous Bohemian castle was lovingly built by a Czech immigrant and mineworker.

name: 'A Precinct For Gods'
description: This Franklinford, Australia site was once home to one of the remotest churches of its faith—in the fictional world of the Kcymaerxthaere.

name: Rabbit Cemetery
description: A unique memorial to the bunnies who died because of Australia's historic Rabbit Proof Fence.

name: Brim Silo Murals

name: Wildflower Brewery
description: Inventive brewers use NSW ingredients to dream up flavorful beers made with local honey, native raspberries, and Mariposa plums.

name: BallaGNOMEia
description: Gnomes have taken over a rest stop on Australia's longest straight road.

name: Albany Chainsaw Sculptures
description: Inside this sculpture park are dozens of woodcrafts crafted by the gentle hands of a chainsaw.

name: Kilcoy
description: This small Australian town is considered home to the "Yowie" or the Aussie Big Foot.

name: Hector Vasyli Memorial
description: A nearly lost tribute to an 11-year-old boy killed during a World War I homecoming parade.

name: Castle Rock
description: A metal skywalk leads to the top of this striking granite outcrop in Porongurup National Park.

name: Griffiths Sea Shell Museum
description: This museum is home to one of the largest private collections of seashells in Australia.

name: Mary Poppins Statue
description: A statue of the beloved storybook character stands in front of the building in which her creator was born.

name: City Park Macaques
description: A small troop of Japanese macaques live peacefully in the heart of this Tasmanian city.

name: 'Alice'
description: The famed literary character has a unique connection to this city.

name: The Big Dish
description: With a surface area of nearly 500 square meters, welcome to the world's largest paraboloidal dish solar concentrator.

name: Murray Bridge Bunyip
description: An animatronic tribute to a legendary half-man, half-fish creature.

name: Maritime Museum of Tasmania

name: The Trugo Mural
description: The artwork celebrates Trugo, a odd yet beloved local sport played only around Melbourne.

name: Dr. Russell's Imaginarium
description: This antique store and museum features unusual items from all corners of the world.

name: Big Cheese
description: Giant wheel of cheese marks a dairying village.

name: Victoria Theatre
description: The oldest remaining theatre in New South Wales.

name: Richmond Bridge
description: It's beautiful, it's haunted and its claim to fame is that it's the oldest existing stone arch bridge in Australia.

name: The Big Easel
description: An oversized Van Gogh reproduction is one of three massive sunflower paintings across the globe.

name: 'Memory Is Creation Without End'
description: This work of art was designed to represent the Tarpeian Way Quarry that once dominated the area.

name: Sansouci Puppet Museum
description: This small town museum is home to the largest collection of puppets in Australia.

name: Taroona Shot Tower
description: The world's tallest shot tower—maybe.

name: Woomera Range Complex
description: Bomb tests and sheep ranchers share the world's largest military land base.

name: Tarra Falls
description: A waterfall rushing through a lush fern gully in Australia's Tarra-Bulga National Park.

name: Ubirr Rock Art
description: Kakadu National Park is known for its stunning beauty and its rock art, which represents the longest historical records of any continuous civilization on Earth.

name: Australian In-Situ Wild Rice Conservation Project
description: A Japanese sculptor carved an ode to biodiversity into a floodplain.

name: 'The Astronomer'
description: A stargazing sculpture made from the ruins of an iconic telescope.

name: Big Gold Panner
description: Hopefully inspecting his tin pan for little nuggets of gold.

name: Swan View Tunnel
description: This 1800s former railway tunnel is now part of a heritage walking trail.

name: Bin Chicken Island
description: This island at Coburg Lake Reserve is home to a sizable, very visible colony of Australian white ibis.

name: Big Miner's Lamp
description: Giant lamp houses the tourism information center.

name: Deni Ute Muster
description: The world's largest gathering of flatbed coupes can be found annually at this rural festival in New South Wales.

name: Diana and the Hounds Fountain
description: A striking bronze sculpture of a Roman goddess with two hound dogs for company.

name: 'Harsh Pasture'

name: Hotel Eastlink
description: A roadside hotel that you can’t stop at.

name: Lightning Ridge Artesian Bore Baths
description: On the outskirts of an opal-mining town, these baths fed by a massive underground aquifer are a natural spa.

name: Jurassic Ark
description: A museum in rural Queensland wrongly claims that dinosaur fossils possess evidence that the earth is only 10,000 years old.

name: Den of Nargun
description: Mind the rock monster at this cave.

name: Harmonie German Club
description: The largest piece of the Berlin Wall in the Southern Hemisphere sits outside this German social club in a Canberra suburb.

name: Big Ant
description: Designed by artist Pro Hart, built elsewhere in 1980.

name: Kylie Tennant's Hut
description: An Australian novelist's cozy coastal retreat, turned campground.

name: The Cascades Female Factory
description: The forgotten and tragic story of the thousands of female convicts forcibly transported to Tasmania is told inside the ruins of this notorious 19th-century workhouse.

name: King Solomon's Cave
description: A limestone cave filled with impressive formations in Mole Creek Karst National Park.

name: Surrey Dive
description: This former brickworks pit is full of legends about its murky depths.

name: Dingo Flour Sign
description: Visible from both land and sea.

name: Charlie the Water Buffalo
description: The taxidermy remains of the mighty beast made famous by a Crocodile Dundee film.

name: The Wall In The Wilderness
description: Carved panels tell the complete history of the harsh Central Highlands region.

name: Big Meat Ant
description: Inspired by the local football team, this giant ant is meant to draw tourists to a tiny Australian town.

name: The Old Umbrella Shop
description: A historical umbrella shop, whose owner started a craze for locally-made souvenirs.

name: Porcupine Flat Gold Dredge and Dragline
description: These two large, rusting pieces of machinery are relics of Australia’s gold mining past.

name: Mr Yakkerboo and Blue
description: Dedicated to a strange, local cryptid beloved by all.

name: Australian-American Memorial
description: From a distance, this statue of an eagle looks like a giant chicken on a stick.

name: Martindale Hall
description: An opulent mansion that acted as the set for Appleyard Girls School in the 1975 classic film 'Picnic at Hanging Rock.'

name: Hou Wang Temple
description: The last timber and iron Chinese temple in Australia.

name: Cistern Chapel
description: These public toilets are a work of art.

name: Walter Taylor Bridge
description: The pillar of this bridge contains a small home and underneath, a ballroom.

name: Berry Deep Lead Gold Mine
description: One of the most successful gold mines of the Victorian gold rush.

name: Busselton Jetty
description: The longest timber-piled jetty in the world has an underwater observatory.

name: Buckley's Cave
description: William Buckley escaped from prison and is said to have spent years hiding in this seaside cave.

name: Ned Kelly's House
description: From this modest childhood home, Ned Kelly went on to become Australia’s most infamous bushranger.

name: Speakers’ Corner

name: Adelaide's Oldest Morris & Co. Stained Glass Window
description: This may be one of the most valuable Morris & Co. stained glass windows in Australia.

name: Backpackers Memorial
description: A somber memorial to the seven victims of a horrible crime.

name: Yarra Bend Asylum Pillar
description: This lone pillar is all that remains of a 19th-century asylum.

name: Heath Ledger Memorial
description: This poignant site pays tribute to one of Australia’s greatest actors.

name: Brewarrina Fish Traps
description: These Aboriginal fish traps are among the oldest human constructions in the world.

name: HMAS Otway
description: The decommissioned submarine is now the symbol of a small, inland Australian town.

name: Ramahyuck Cemetery
description: At Ramahyuck, the Gunaikurnai people were forced to trade their freedom and culture for protection. Today, a cemetery is all that remains of the site.

name: Magnetic Termite Mounds
description: Let these towering termite-building structures tell you where to go.

name: Broulee Canoe Tree
description: This tree, with its large oval-shaped scar, is a rare piece of Aboriginal cultural history.

name: Big Lizzie
description: One of the world's largest tractors was built to replace camel caravans.

name: Tom Edwards Memorial Fountain
description: A monument to a working class martyr.

name: Rainbow Sea Container
description: This work of public art symbolizes the maritime history and the modern creative industry in Fremantle.

name: 'Savior of a Friend'

name: Giant Readymix Logo

name: Buckley's Bunyip
description: A tribute to one man's encounter with a frightening cryptid.

name: The Gap and Natural Bridge
description: Wild seas have created two spectacular formations just a stone's throw away from each other.

name: Devil's Terror Cutting
description: A feat of railway engineering required to bypass a muddy quagmire.

name: New Italy
description: Once a farming community founded by the Italian survivors of an immigration scam, this colony is now a free camp and roadside attraction.

name: QDOS Arts
description: A tranquil gallery and sculpture garden situated within the classic Australian bush.

name: The Welcome Stranger Monument
description: This stone obelisk commemorates the discovery of the world's largest gold nugget.

name: Melbourne Rectangular Stadium

name: Lake Macdonnell
description: This salt lake was a former mine site and now attracts visitors for its remarkable pink colors.

name: Bon Scott's Grave
description: The final resting place of this rock icon has become a pilgrimage site for AC/DC fans.

name: The Big Camera

name: Cloudland Memorial Arch
description: This arch celebrates the romantic memories of those who danced at the Cloudland Ballroom.

name: 'Hongsedaunt'

name: Sovereign Hill
description: This outdoor museum harkens back to the height of the gold rush.

name: Heatherlie Quarry

name: 'Citizens Gateway to the Great Barrier Reef'
description: This public art is inspired by a local Aboriginal creation story.

name: Bulgandry Art Site Aboriginal Place
description: Aboriginal artists carved these unique figures into sandstone.

name: Walgun / Cape Byron

name: The Matilda

name: Eight-Hour-Day Monument

name: 'Anmatjere Man'

name: Goldfields Water Supply Scheme
description: An engineering masterpiece, or “Scheme of Madness?”

name: 'Heading South' Statues at Hobart Harbour

name: Robe Chinese Monument

name: Australian Nurses Memorial

name: Wadjemup Lighthouse
description: Ships still use this historic lighthouse to navigate dangerous, island waters.

name: All Saints Anglican Church
description: This church's unusual design comes from its previous life as a mortuary station.

name: Wellington Dam
description: This 86,000-square-foot artwork is the largest mural in the world to cover a dam.

name: Toora Dog Tree
description: A carved tribute to these artist's furry friends.

name: Canistrum Toilets
description: An iconic designer dunny inspired by Aboriginal fishing baskets.

name: Sun Pictures
description: The oldest operating picture gardens in the world.

name: Lake Eacham

name: Alex T. Brown Shipwreck
description: The last vestiges of this ship are mostly buried beneath the sand dunes.

name: De Salis Cemetery
description: A rare and unique 19th-century pastoral burial site elevated off the ground.

name: Black Rock Stakes Sculpture
description: This sculpture honors the legacy of Australia's now-defunct, Black Rock Stakes wheelbarrow race.

name: Skipping Girl Sign
description: Saved by the community from destruction, this sign has become a national treasure.

name: Perth Observatory
description: The oldest observatory in West Australia.

name: Mawson's Huts Replica Museum
description: The world's only full-size replica of the research hut used by Douglas Mawson for his 1911-14 Australasian Antarctic Expedition.

name: Robert Burns Statue
description: A statue of a Scottish cultural icon in central Sydney.

name: Wirth's Circus Mosaic
description: Designed to celebrate more than a century of circus performances in Australia.

name: 'Mullum Mullum the Owl'
description: A sculpture of the largest owl in Australia.

name: Murtoa Stick Shed

name: Denmark Fairy Doors Trail
description: This magical trail takes visitors on a journey round miniature wooden houses on the picturesque Denmark River.

name: Echuca Wharf
description: A timber wharf that contributed to Echuca becoming the largest inland port in 19th-century Australia.

name: The Black Stump
description: The oft used Australian colloquialism "Beyond the Black Stump" is said to have originated at this location.

name: ‘Summertime’ Dog Sculpture
description: A tribute to the local dog beach.

name: Australian Standing Stones
description: A tribute to the Celtic nations that helped settle Australia.

name: Old Nullarbor Roadhouse
description: This restored station garage allows visitors to step back in time at this remote outpost on the Nullarbor Plain.

name: The Steppes Sculptures
description: These bronze sculptures can be found in an isolated patch of bush in Tasmania’s highlands.

name: Robert Penney Bakery Sculpture
description: A 19th-century baker stands ready to serve customers.

name: Jimmy Possum Chair
description: A faceless folk hero and his legacy of comfortable, expertly-crafted chairs lives on in a small Tasmanian town.

name: Northern Cattle Market Wall
description: A unique reminder of the once-bustling cattle market during the 19th-century.

name: COURAGE Statue
description: This sculpture was designed to encourage viewers to be true to themselves.

name: Glenaladale Weir
description: The remains of a failed attempt to regulate Mitchell River.

name: Cresta Valley Ski Resort
description: This Australian ski resort was forced to close due to changing climate conditions.

name: The Three Lost Children Walk & Monument
description: A memorial to one of the saddest stories in Victoria's history.

name: Gowar School Ruins
description: This school was established during the height of the Australian Gold Rush.

name: Yanakie Station Cemetery
description: Only five people are buried in this tiny cemetery in Wilsons Promontory National Park.

name: Snowy River Railway Bridge
description: When it was built in 1916, this bridge was critical infrastructure for local farmers. Today, it's being restored for walking and biking.

name: Rubicon Hydroelectric Scheme
description: One of the oldest operating hydroelectric power schemes in Australia.

name: Emu Rising Mosaic
description: This public work of art pays tribute to an early Aboriginal Australian constellation pattern.

name: La Trobe's Cottage
description: The former home of Victoria's first Lieutenant-Governor.

name: Childers Backpacker Memorial
description: This former hostel is now an art gallery and a memorial for 15 backpackers who lost their lives to a tragic act of arson.

name: Ferenc Puskás Memorial
description: A bronze statue pays tribute to the Hungarian player and coach, one of soccer's first international superstars.

name: Burke and Wills Monument
description: This unusual stone monument marks the starting point of the epic and tragic event.

name: Murphy’s Haystacks
description: These oddly shaped pink granite formations got their name after being mistaken for massive piles of hay.

name: Cossack Historic Town
description: This once-thriving coastal area is now a preserved ghost town.

name: Ben Chifley's Model Airplane
description: The intriguing artifact can be found in the former Australian prime minister's home.

name: D'Arenberg Cube
description: This green and white building is shaped like a massive Rubik's Cube.

name: The World's Tallest Bin
description: Although not officially the world's tallest bin, this giant trash receptacle was designed to stop littering.

name: Penong Windmill Museum
description: A tiny town's spinning, whirling propeller collection holds Australia's largest windmill.

name: 'Brig Amity'
description: A replica of an early 19th-century vessel that sailed across the world.

name: Grave of Daniel "Mad Dog" Morgan
description: The burial site of one of the most notorious bushrangers in Australia's history.

name: Grave of Walter Lindrum
description: A distinctive monument for this former world champion takes the form of a billiards table, complete with balls and cue.

name: 1972 Taroom Truck Explosion Memorial
description: One of the biggest mysteries of Central Queensland: How did this truck spontaneously combust with the force of a massive bomb?

name: King Leonidas Statue
description: This statue commemorates a special connection between ancient Greece and the Melbourne suburb of Brunswick.

name: Stanbury Jacko
description: A stuffed, prize-winning ram is all that remains of a world-renowned stud line, and a reminder of its owner's gruesome end.

name: 'Catch Me'
description: Designed by an artist known for his eccentric, satirical public works of art.

name: Daylesford Convent
description: This former convent has been reimagined as a community center for artists.

name: Woomera Ground Zero for British Atomic Tests
description: This remote site in the Australian Outback is where the British government conducted atomic bomb tests in the 1950s.

name: Spirit Dog Sculptures
description: This public work of art outside of Perth depicts a dog walker and several mischievous canine companions.

name: Temple of Peace
description: A most unusual war memorial, erected by some most unusual people, made of concrete and stained glass, and apparently honouring five people and a dog.

name: Semaphore Time Ball Tower
description: This stone tower was built in the late 19th century to signal the time to ships in the water.

name: New Australasian No. 2 Deep Lead Gold Mine
description: The site of a tragic mining disaster in 1882.

name: Banana the Bullock
description: A fallen bull's legacy lives on in the rural cattle town that is named after him.

name: Tasman Fountain
description: Located alongside the famous Salamanca Market and in sight of Parliament House, this large and elaborate monument is hard to miss, split into two sections and standing some 14 meters tall.

name: Glen Huntly Pioneers Memorial
description: Built to commemorate the lives of hopeful emigrants who died on a journey from Scotland to Australia.

name: Floating Sauna Lake Derby
description: Warm up from the freezing waters in this fully functional sauna in the middle of the Tasmanian wilderness.

name: Dalhousie Springs
description: Swim in these hot springs filled with three-million-year-old water, deep in the Australian Outback.

name: Water Wheel - Cape Leeuwin
description: This water wheel turned to stone in a matter of decades.

name: Ningaloo George
description: A fibreglass sculpture of a whale shark on the grounds of a petrol station.

name: The Little Grave
description: This lonely gravestone beside an auto parts store is a reminder of the hardships of a pioneering past.

name: Broome Japanese Cemetery
description: The final resting place for many Japanese divers who tragically lost their lives in the Australian pearling industry.

name: The West Australian Irish Famine Memorial
description: A tribute to those who fled the Great Famine and relocated to Australia.

name: Rundle Lantern
description: An iconic work of public art that lights up the downtown area.

name: Charlie's Aeroplane
description: One man’s legacy soars high on the wings of a miniature plane.

name: Site of the World's Tallest Tree
description: The massive mountain ash that once stood at this site was cut down in an effort to prove that it was, indeed, the tallest of its kind.

name: J Ward Museum
description: Now a museum, this was once a prison facility and a hospital for the “criminally insane.”

name: Caiguna Blowhole
description: One of the breathing caves of Australia's Nullarbor Plain.

name: Errol Flynn Reserve
description: This small seaside reserve supposedly inspired a young Errol Flynn to take the nautical adventures that would define him on and off the silver screen.

name: Boyd's Tower
description: Once a welcome symbol for maritime travelers entering Edrom.

name: Red Dog Grave Memorial
description: This site pays tribute to the life of an iconic local dog.

name: Wauba Debar’s Grave
description: The burial place of this Tasmanian Aboriginal woman is a memorial that remembers the atrocities of colonialism.

name: Kerosene Tin Hut
description: These tin huts were commonplace in Australia during the Great Depression.

name: 'Vault'
description: This abstract sculpture was initially so disliked that it got moved to the outskirts of town a few months after installation.

name: Kurth Kiln
description: This kiln was erected to produce charcoal for gas producer units during World War II.

name: La Trobe Swing Bridge
description: The La Trobe Swing Bridge was the first movable bridge constructed in Victoria.

name: Wurrwurrwuy Stone Arrangements
description: This small but unique site depicts part of an arguably underrepresented part of Australia's early connections to the seafaring communities to its north.

name: Fremantle Prison
description: One of the world’s oldest surviving convict prisons, once the scene of daring escapes and riots.

name: Cyril Callister Museum
description: Housed in a refurbished gas station, this small museum is a tribute to the inventor of Vegemite.

name: Yarrangobilly Caves Thermal Pool
description: A naturally heated pool steaming in the wild heart of Kosciuszko National Park.

name: Red Dog Sculpture
description: This bronze memorial pays tribute to a local dog.

name: Pastor Sir Douglas & Lady Gladys Nicholls Memorial
description: This 2008 memorial commemorates two leading Aboriginal Australian rights activists.

name: Foy & Gibson Factory
description: The Collingwood production facility for a department store that was among the largest and earliest in Australia.

name: Wilmot Novelty Letterbox Trail
description: In Tasmania's "Valley of Views," dozens of novelty mailboxes add a quirky element to the landscape.

name: Ben Prior Park
description: One of the largest open-air museums in Western Australia.

name: Black Swan Hotel
description: An unassuming pub with a huge collection of rare Indian motorcycles.

name: Canberra Holodomor Memorial
description: A memorial that honors the victims of a tragedy in Ukraine.

name: Qantas Founders Museum
description: The museum describes the story of how an Australian airline with a single biplane became one of the biggest in the world.

name: Ballandean Dinosaur
description: Originally made for a fruit festival in the area in 1998 this was then erected outside the railway station and is now one of Australia’s iconic “Big Things” right at the side of the highway.

name: The Phoenix Tree
description: The trunk and roots of a river red gum tree were used to create this art piece.

name: Kurana Aeroplane Crash Memorial
description: A monument commemorating the lives lost when a plane crashed into Mount Macedon in 1948.

name: 'Footsteps Towards Freedom'
description: Three women and a 10-year-old boy are immortalized in this sculpture memorializing the 13,000 women and children convicts sent to Van Diemen’s Land (now Tasmania) between 1803 and 1853.

name: Derby Tunnel
description: Explore the remnants of a 19th-century tin mining operation and get a glimpse into Tasmania's industrial past.

name: Villa Alba Museum
description: Inside this historical home are two panoramic murals of Sydney Harbour and Edinburgh.

name: Cactoblastis Memorial Cairn
description: A monument to the moth that saved Australia from a cactus crisis.

name: Lt. James Cook Monument Cairn
description: A cairn commemorating James Cook's second landing in Australia.

name: Cameron Corner
description: The point where New South Wales meets Queensland and South Australia.

name: The Big Wheelbarrow
description: A super-sized wheelbarrow that sits at the roadside.

name: Mount Tarrengower Lookout Tower
description: A timber transport car and a team of horses were needed to carry this lookout to the top of Mount Tarrengower in the 1920s.

name: Coonalpyn Silo Mural
description: A 100-foot-tall mural in South Australia.

name: ‘Charles Edward 1864’
description: This work of art pays tribute to an iron-hulled paddled steamer that traveled the world.

name: Tin Camel Roundabout
description: A tribute to the role camels played in the region's industrial development.

name: Headless King George V
description: This statue of King George V was beheaded as a protest of colonialism.

name: Centenary of Gold Discovery Monument
description: To commemorate a century of gold mining, this Australian city built a monument that features a replica of a 150-pound gold nugget found there in 1858.

name: Central Deborah Gold Mine
description: Direct access to a gold mine showing the boom and bust of Australia's Victorian gold rush.

name: 'Australian Farmer'
description: A representation of the region's rich agrarian history.

name: Lynton Convict Hiring Depot
description: This remote outpost hosts the remains of one of Australia's key sites in the history of convicted laborers.

name: Sesquipedalian Sea Squirt
description: A controversial interactive sculpture that "weeps" water.

name: Widgiemooltha Golden Eagle Nugget
description: A roadside replica of the largest single piece of gold ever found in Western Australia.

name: Kyeema Memorial
description: A poignant monument dedicated to a tragic 1930s plane crash.

name: 'Mr Poetry'
description: A tribute to local poet and music journalist Adrian Rawlins.

name: Giant Wool Wagon
description: This large replica wagon tells the story of the booming wool industry in this small Australian town.

name: Esperance Stonehenge
description: This is a full-scale replica of a complete Stonehenge made from locally quarried Esperance granite.

name: Mount Buffalo Chalet
description: A symbol of the early days of skiing in Australia

name: Remarkable Rocks
description: Impressively shaped, perfectly balanced boulders perched on the edge of a sea-cliff.

name: Bamboo Creek Tin Mine
description: This site contains preserved ruins and industrial remnants of Western Australia's mining heritage.

name: Dr James Ramsay Fountain
description: This Australian drinking fountain pays tribute to the generosity and talent of a 19th-century Scottish doctor.

name: Ringwood Mining Historical Site
description: A monument commemorating the antimony mine that once operated in this Melbourne suburb.

name: Nhill Draught Horse Memorial
description: Dedicated to one of the horses that helped cultivate the region.

name: Charles Grimes Expedition Cairn
description: A neat pile of shale marks an early English trip to the Yarra River.

name: Balladonia Heritage Museum
description: A chunk of Skylab debris adorns this small museum at a roadside motel in Western Australia.

name: Captain Thunderbolt Statue
description: The last of the bushrangers in New South Wales.

name: Davidson Whaling Station
description: The site where whalers teamed up with orcas to hunt baleen whales.

name: Bishop Rosendo Salvado Statue
description: Dedicated to the founder of "New Norcia."

name: Fremantle Submarine Periscope Memorial
description: The original attack periscope from a 1940s navy submarine towers high at this memorial site.

name: Condamine Bell
description: A giant version of an Australian town's claim to fame: a cowbell said to be audible from miles away.

name: Albany's Historic Whaling Station
description: Home to the last shore-based whaling station in Australia.

name: Jacob’s Ladder
description: Western Australia’s heavenly staircase is not for the faint of heart.

name: Hamelin Pool Telegraph Station
description: This remote historic museum tells the story of the early telecommunications systems in 1800s Australia.

name: John Duigan Monument
description: Dedicated to the first flight of an aircraft to be designed and built in Australia.

name: A.A. Cassimatis General Store Museum
description: Stocks of hardware, automotive supplies, crockery, and farm machinery are on display as though time had simply stopped at some point in the 1930s.

name: James Irvine Monument
description: Dedicated to a pivotal figure in the region's tourism industry.

name: Alexandra Battery
description: A hidden gem of Hobart’s past, now a park with panoramic views and plenty of history.

name: Circular Pool
description: A forest pool so dark and frothy it's earned the nickname the natural cappuccino.

name: Springthorpe Memorial
description: A hauntingly beautiful grave commemorates love evermore.

name: Deakin Anticline
description: This fascinating and unusual geological formation dominates a suburb of Canberra.

name: Yilgarn History Museum
description: This small museum celebrates the history of an Australian town known as the "Gateway to the Goldfields."

name: The Wreck Bell
description: This bell was once used to summon rescue crews when ships fell victim to the treacherous waterways.

name: Tunnel at Tunnel
description: This tunnel gave a name to a tiny community, but is now abandoned.

name: Eliza Sculpture
description: A beloved statue of a diving woman in Perth’s Swan River is frequently dressed up in costumes for special occasions.

name: HMVS Victoria Swivel Link
description: All that remains of the first colonial naval ship in Australia is located in a park in Queenscliff.

name: John Mills Himself
description: This businessman wanted to make a name for himself (and no one else).

name: Frankie the Chrome Gnome
description: A 9-metre tall reflective steel statue of a garden gnome crafted by Gregor Kregar, a New Zealand-Serbian artist.

name: Lionel Rose Statue
description: A monument to the first Aboriginal Australian to win a world boxing title.

name: Strauss Airstrip
description: This preserved site served as part of Northern Australia’s defense in World War II.

name: Tomb of the Dunbar Victims
description: This cemetery holds the unidentified remains from a wreck sometimes known as “Australia’s Titanic.”

name: Port Melbourne Lighthouses
description: A pair of beacons from the 1920s guide ships steering a course to Melbourne.

name: Mount Macedon Memorial Cross
description: A tribute to the fallen of World War I now honors all who have lost their lives to war.

name: 1940 Canberra Air Disaster Memorial
description: This memorial marks the site of not only of a tragic wreck, but an event that led to the downfall of a wartime government.

name: Dangar Island
description: A unique car-free island in the Hawkesbuy River serviced by a regular ferry and on-island electric buggy.

name: Grave of Andrew John Phillips
description: According to a plaque at this memorial, "he could swim, but his horse could not."

name: Staff Nurse Elizabeth Rothery Memorial
description: Dedicated to the 63 local women who served the country during World War I.

name: George Bass Expedition Memorial
description: Dedicated to a surgeon who made several natural discoveries across Australia.

name: Myrtleford Old School Museum
description: An insight into the town's history of tobacco farming and involvement in World War II.

name: Port Denison Fishermen’s Memorial
description: A poignant site that pays tribute to lives lost at sea.

name: Mid North Coast Fire Museum
description: A volunteer-run museum dedicated to past and present firefighting practices.

name: Prime Ministers Avenue
description: A collection of busts honor the first 29 prime ministers of Australia.

name: Gas Stripping Tower
description: A heritage-listed, cast-iron tower that was used for stripping out components of raw coal gas.

name: Lord McAlpine Statue
description: A bronze sculpture dedicated a man who transformed the town into a modern tourist destination.

name: The Piano Tuner’s Grave
description: A grave commemorates piano tuner F. W. Schlieffen, who drowned in floodwaters in 1906.

name: The Haunted Bookshop
description: Occult books, tarot, oddities.

name: Larundel Mental Asylum
description: Dilapidated ruins that once treated a notorious young serial killer, currently hosting vandals, squatters, and ghost hunters.

name: The Lillipad Cafe
description: An indigenous ethnobotanist runs this brunch spot celebrating Australia’s native flavors.

name: Wycliffe Well
description: The UFO capital of Australia is the outback’s answer to Roswell.

name: Logical Unsanity Books & Miscellaneous Phantasmagoria
description: This rickety old shed hides a trove of secondhand literary treasures.

name: Olympia Milk Bar
description: This odd 1930s shop remains stranded in time and shrouded in mystery.

name: Yerrabingin Farm
description: The world's first indigenous rooftop farm boasts 2,000 plants from more than 35 different native Australian species.

name: One More Go One More Go
description: Outdoor art exhibition made of classic Tetris pieces.

name: The Departure
description: An art gallery, studio, and shop that celebrates death.

name: Giant Earthworm Museum
description: "The Wonder From Down Under," a worm shaped museum celebrating the endangered Giant Gippsland earthworm.

name: Giant Mushroom
description: Children's playground hidden under a big pink and blue cap.

name: Big Orange
description: Just one of many oranges on this citrus-happy continent.

name: Kennel Road
description: Plastic pups and their colorful homes greet travelers on this otherwise ordinary Australian street.

name: 'Brunswick Street'
description: The artist behind this historical depiction of transportation remains a mystery.
